label: Srinagar
sub: Capital of Indian-administered J&K. Target of Pakistani tribal invasion in 1947; saved by Indian Army airlift. Epicenter of the Kashmir insurgency since 1989.

label: Muzaffarabad
sub: Capital of Azad Kashmir (Pakistan-administered). First major town captured by Pakistani tribal forces in October 1947. Administrative center of Pakistan's side of the Kashmir divide.

label: New Delhi
sub: Capital of India. Site of Indian Parliament attack by LeT/JeM militants (December 13, 2001). Major military command decisions including Operation Vijay and Balakot airstrike authorized here.

label: Islamabad
sub: Capital of Pakistan (built 1960s, replacing Karachi). GHQ is here; ISI headquarters nearby. Center of Pakistani strategic decision-making on Kashmir and nuclear policy.

label: Lahore
sub: Pakistan's cultural capital and second-largest city. Indian forces advanced toward Lahore in September 1965 war. Lahore Declaration (1999) signed here during Vajpayee's historic bus trip.

label: Siachen Glacier
sub: World's highest battlefield (5,000–6,200m). Seized by India in Operation Meghdoot (April 1984). Both armies maintain permanent posts here; more soldiers have died from cold and avalanches than combat.

label: Pokhran Test Site
sub: India's nuclear test range in Rajasthan desert. Site of Smiling Buddha test (May 1974) and five Pokhran-II tests (May 1998). Area remains off-limits to civilians.

label: Chagai Hills (Pakistan Tests)
sub: Pakistan's nuclear test site in Balochistan. Site of five nuclear detonations on May 28, 1998 (Operation Chagai-I) and one on May 30 (Chagai-II), in response to India's Pokhran-II tests.

label: Kahuta — Khan Research Labs
sub: Pakistan's primary uranium enrichment facility, established 1976 by A.Q. Khan using centrifuge technology stolen from URENCO. Heart of Pakistan's nuclear weapons program.

label: Kargil
sub: Town in Ladakh, India. Site of the 1999 Kargil War. Pakistani forces occupied surrounding peaks; Indian forces recaptured them in Operation Vijay (May–July 1999).

label: Tiger Hill (Point 5062)
sub: Strategic Kargil peak overlooking Srinagar-Leh highway. Recaptured by Indian 18 Grenadiers regiment on July 4, 1999 — key turning point of the Kargil War.

label: Drass
sub: World's second coldest inhabited place (-60°C in winter). Theater of intense fighting in Kargil War 1999. Pakistan's NLI troops had occupied nearby peaks including Point 5353 and Tololing.

label: Wagah-Attari Border
sub: Only operational road border crossing between India and Pakistan. Famous for daily flag-lowering ceremony. Closed to trade after Pulwama attack 2019; reopened partially and closed again after Pahalgam 2025.

label: Balakot Airstrike Target
sub: Jaish-e-Mohammed training facility in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan, struck by IAF Mirage 2000 jets on February 26, 2019. First Indian airstrike inside Pakistan proper since 1971 war.

label: Pulwama Attack Site
sub: Location on Jammu-Srinagar highway where JeM suicide bomber struck CRPF convoy on February 14, 2019, killing 40 personnel. Deadliest militant attack in Kashmir in three decades.

label: Uri Army Base
sub: Indian Army Brigade HQ at Uri, Baramulla attacked by four JeM militants on September 18, 2016, killing 19 soldiers. Attack triggered India's surgical strikes across LOC 11 days later.

label: Pathankot Air Force Station
sub: Attacked by 6 JeM gunmen on January 2, 2016. 72-hour siege killed 7 Indian security personnel; all attackers killed. Attack weeks after Modi's surprise visit to Lahore.

label: Taj Mahal Palace Hotel (Mumbai 26/11)
sub: Primary siege site during November 26–29, 2008 Mumbai attacks. LeT gunmen held the hotel for three days; Indian NSG commandos eventually cleared it. 31 people killed at the Taj.

label: Mumbai CST Station (26/11)
sub: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus railway station, attacked by Ajmal Kasab and Abu Ismail during 26/11. At least 58 people were killed in the station attack. Kasab was later captured at nearby Cama Hospital road.

label: Nariman House / Chabad House (26/11)
sub: Jewish community center in Mumbai besieged during 26/11 attacks. Rabbi Gavriel Holtzberg, his wife Rivka, and four others were killed by two LeT gunmen over two days.

label: Muridke — Lashkar-e-Taiba HQ
sub: Town north of Lahore housing the massive Markaz Taiba complex — headquarters of Lashkar-e-Taiba (operating as Jamaat-ud-Dawah). Struck by India in Operation Sindoor (May 2025).

label: Bahawalpur — Jaish-e-Mohammed Base
sub: City in Punjab, Pakistan, home to Jaish-e-Mohammed's Markaz Subhan Allah complex. Targeted by India's Operation Sindoor strikes on May 7, 2025.

label: LOC — Salamabad Uri Sector
sub: Active LOC sector near Uri where Pakistani tribal forces entered Kashmir in 1947. Site of numerous ceasefire violations and cross-LOC infiltration attempts over 75+ years.

label: Jammu
sub: Winter capital of J&K. Strategic transit hub and airbase. Site of multiple militant attacks over the years. Connected to Srinagar by the high-altitude Banihal Pass and Jawahar Tunnel.

label: Gilgit-Baltistan
sub: Pakistan-administered territory claimed by India as part of J&K. India views any development projects here, including CPEC, as violation of its sovereignty. Upgraded to quasi-provincial status by Pakistan in 2020.

label: Sialkot Sector (1965 War)
sub: Scene of the largest tank battle since World War II during the September 1965 war. India's 1st Armoured Division clashed with Pakistan's 6 Armoured Division. India crossed the international border here.

label: Lahore Sector (1965 War)
sub: Indian forces advanced to within kilometers of Lahore in September 1965 before the UN ceasefire. Both sides suffered heavy armor losses in the Kasur-Lahore corridor.

label: Dhaka (Surrender Site, 1971)
sub: Capital where Pakistani forces surrendered on December 16, 1971. The joint India-Bangladesh victory led to creation of Bangladesh. Pakistan's Lt. Gen. Niazi signed the surrender to Indian Lt. Gen. Aurora.

label: Karachi
sub: Pakistan's financial capital and largest city. Pakistan's first capital. Karachi Agreement (1949) formalizing the LOC signed here. Indian Navy blockaded Karachi harbor during 1971 war.

label: Neelum Valley LOC
sub: Pakistani Neelum Valley closely parallels the LOC. Artillery exchanges along this valley have been common; Indian positions overlook Pakistani-side roads and towns.

label: Tololing (Point 4590)
sub: First major Kargil peak recaptured by India, on June 13, 1999. Indian Army's 2 Rajputana Rifles assaulted the fortified peak multiple times before taking it. Critical to reopening the Srinagar-Leh highway.

label: Rawalpindi — Pakistan GHQ
sub: Headquarters of Pakistan Army (General Headquarters). Center of Pakistan's military-strategic decision-making. Location of General Musharraf's coup against Nawaz Sharif (October 1999).

label: Peshawar
sub: Capital of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. Major hub for Afghan refugees and mujahideen during Soviet-Afghan War, which spilled militants into Kashmir. APS School massacre (Dec 2014) killed 132 children.

label: Khunjerab Pass (China-Pakistan Border)
sub: World's highest paved international border crossing at 4,693m. Entry point of China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC). India views CPEC as violation of its sovereignty over Gilgit-Baltistan.

label: NJ9842 — LOC Northern Terminus
sub: Where the 1949 LOC formally ends. Its description 'thence north to the glaciers' was the ambiguity Pakistan exploited to justify Siachen claims; India pre-empted by occupying the glacier in 1984.

label: Chamb Sector
sub: Scene of Pakistan's Operation Grand Slam offensive in September 1965 and an offensive in December 1971. Pakistan made significant gains here in 1971; the area changed hands multiple times.

label: Indian Parliament — December 2001 Attack
sub: Five LeT/JeM militants attacked the Indian Parliament complex on December 13, 2001, killing 9 security personnel. The attack triggered Operation Parakram — India's largest military mobilization since 1971.

label: Amarnath Shrine
sub: Sacred Hindu cave shrine in Anantnag district, Kashmir. Annual pilgrimages have been targeted by militants multiple times. Attack on pilgrims in July 2017 killed 7 and reignited LOC tensions.

label: Sopore
sub: Town in Baramulla district known for intense militant activity during the 1990s insurgency. Scene of numerous encounters and human rights incidents. Apple-growing region whose economy was devastated by conflict.

label: Kabul (India-Pakistan Proxy Competition)
sub: India invested $3B+ in Afghanistan post-2001 in infrastructure and development, competing with Pakistani strategic influence. Taliban return in 2021 was a setback for Indian influence. Pakistan historically supported Taliban as strategic depth against India.

label: Aksai Chin (Disputed — China/India)
sub: Disputed territory claimed by India as part of Ladakh but controlled by China since 1962. China built a strategic road through Aksai Chin. India's bilateral disputes with both China and Pakistan create a two-front strategic challenge.

label: Shaksgam Valley (Pakistan-China Cession)
sub: Area of Gilgit-Baltistan ceded by Pakistan to China in 1963. India considers this an illegal transfer of sovereign Indian territory. Pakistan and China jointly administer the area.

label: Pahalgam — Baisaran Meadow Attack (2025)
sub: Tourists attacked at Baisaran meadow, Pahalgam, on April 22, 2025, killing 26 people. LeT-linked Resistance Front initially claimed responsibility. Triggered India's Indus Waters Treaty suspension and Operation Sindoor.

label: Leh (Kushok Bakula Rimpochhe Airport)
sub: India's key high-altitude airbase at 3,256m, vital for supplying Siachen Glacier and Ladakh forces. Major logistics hub that enabled Indian success in Kargil War. Significantly expanded after 2020 Galwan clash.

label: Skardu Airbase (Pakistan)
sub: Pakistan's major military airbase in Gilgit-Baltistan, used to supply Siachen forces and Kargil positions. Capable of handling fighter aircraft; strategic base contested in the Siachen conflict.

label: Poonch LOC Sector
sub: Active LOC sector in Poonch district. Site of numerous infiltration attempts and cross-border artillery exchanges. Indian forces besieged here in 1947–48 before relief. Civilian pockets surround the LOC.

label: Rajouri — LOC Zone
sub: Rajouri district frequently targeted by militants crossing LOC. Multiple IED blasts and militant encounters in 2023–2024. India stepped up counter-insurgency operations in this Jammu region.

label: Daulat Beg Oldi (DBO)
sub: World's highest airstrip (5,006m). Operational Indian airbase near China's Aksai Chin and Siachen Glacier. C-130J Hercules aircraft landed here in 2013; IAF uses it for logistics to Siachen. Critical strategic asset.

label: Khushab Nuclear Complex
sub: Pakistan's plutonium production complex with four heavy water reactors. Enables production of weapons-grade plutonium for Pakistan's growing nuclear arsenal. SIPRI tracks expansion of these facilities.

label: BARC Trombay (India Nuclear Research)
sub: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre — India's primary nuclear research complex. Developed the reprocessing technology used in Smiling Buddha test (1974). Also develops civilian nuclear technology.

label: Abbottabad
sub: Pakistani city where Osama bin Laden was killed by US Navy SEALs in May 2011. Proximity to PMA Kakul (Pakistan's military academy) raised questions about Pakistani military's knowledge of his presence.

label: Srinagar Airbase
sub: Vital Indian Air Force base in Kashmir. First Indian troops airlifted here on October 27, 1947 to defend Srinagar. Used for IAF strikes during Kargil War. Strategic base continuously upgraded.

label: Agra — India-Pakistan Summit (2001)
sub: Site of the July 2001 Agra Summit between Vajpayee and Musharraf that failed to produce a joint declaration. The talks collapsed over Kashmir vs. terrorism sequencing dispute.

label: Tashkent (Soviet-Mediated 1966 Peace)
sub: Capital of Uzbekistan (then USSR) where India-Pakistan Tashkent Declaration was signed on January 10, 1966, ending the 1965 war. Indian PM Shastri died of heart attack hours after signing.

label: Keran LOC Sector
sub: Remote LOC sector in Kupwara district, used as infiltration route by militants crossing from Pakistan into Kashmir. Multiple counter-insurgency operations in the dense forests here.

label: Rann of Kutch (1965 Skirmish)
sub: Pakistan-India skirmish in this salt marsh border region in April 1965. Pakistan made territorial gains. UK-brokered ceasefire followed. Pakistan interpreted India's restrained response as weakness, triggering Operation Gibraltar.

label: International Border — Punjab Sector
sub: The India-Pakistan International Border in Punjab (not the LOC). India crossed this in September 1965 and December 1971. Scene of India-Pakistan military mobilizations including Operation Parakram (2001–02) and post-Pulwama tensions.

label: Hyderabad, Sindh
sub: Major Pakistani city where Indian forces halted advance during 1971 war in Sindh sector. Not to be confused with Indian Hyderabad. Site of Pakistani defensive line in southern theater of 1971 conflict.

label: Leopold Café (Mumbai 26/11)
sub: Popular tourist café in Mumbai's Colaba attacked in the first wave of the 26/11 attacks by Ajmal Kasab and Abu Ismail. 10 killed. The attack coordinated with simultaneous strikes at CST, Taj, and Oberoi.

label: Kupwara — Primary Infiltration District
sub: Kupwara district in northern Kashmir is the most common entry point for militants crossing from Pakistan-administered Kashmir. Numerous encounters, seizes of weapons caches, and counter-infiltration operations here.
